methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Captura is a Series A climate & energy company founded by Harry Atwater and CX Xiang, having raised $30M+ to date. The valuation universe consists of 9 private peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including later-stage companies such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $30M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $60M. This reflects typical venture step-up dynamics from Series A to subsequent rounds. The peer-median method is not applicable: while 9 same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints meet narrow stage or valuation-tier filters (fewer than 3 comparables), making a narrow peer median unreliable. A broad-sector median would obscure the heterogeneity within Climate & Energy and introduce undue noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust peer set at equivalent stage and the lack of disclosed metrics on Captura's technical maturity, IP position, or customer traction. A more granular valuation would benefit from additional late-stage Climate & Energy comparables or internal operational milestones that distinguish Captura's position within the sector.
